visualization.title: Enrollments per Semester and Academic Year

resultRaw.sql: SELECT cs.semester_type AS semester, cs.academic_year AS academic_year, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count FROM public.enrollments e JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id WHERE e.is_active = true AND cs.semester_type IS NOT NULL AND cs.academic_year IS NOT NULL GROUP BY cs.semester_type, cs.academic_year ORDER BY cs.academic_year ASC, cs.semester_type ASC;

issues: academic_year is treated as a nominal (string) field — lexicographic sorting (sort: "ascending") may not be chronological; consider parsing/normalizing academic_year or providing an explicit sort order if you need true chronological order.
semester has no explicit sort/domain — if you require a specific semester order (e.g., SPRING, SUMMER, FALL), set an ordinal domain for the color/xOffset scale.
If the intent was to count unique students per semester rather than enrollment rows, replace COUNT(e.id) with COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id).
